name: Heroku Platform API
what_it_does: Salesforce-owned PaaS for deploying web apps via git with dyno-based compute, managed add-ons (Postgres, Redis), and a rich ecosystem of third-party add-ons.
use_cases: Deploying full-stack web applications with automatic scaling via dyno formation
Managing app lifecycle (create, deploy, scale, restart) programmatically via API
Provisioning and managing database add-ons (Heroku Postgres, Redis) via API
Running background workers and scheduled tasks with dyno types
Legacy app hosting where Heroku buildpack ecosystem simplifies deployment
not_for: Cost-sensitive workloads (Heroku is significantly more expensive than alternatives)
Applications needing GPU compute or specialized hardware
Teams wanting latest infrastructure innovation (Heroku has fallen behind competitors)
best_when: You have existing Heroku-deployed apps and need API-driven management, or are in Salesforce ecosystem where Heroku integration is native.
avoid_when: Starting a new project — Railway, Render, or Fly.io offer better value, performance, and features for equivalent or lower cost.

auth.notes: API key via Authorization: Bearer header. OAuth for third-party integrations with scope-based access. Direct authorization header also accepted.

pricing.paid_tiers: Eco: $5/month for shared dynos; Basic: $7/month; Standard-1X: $25/month; Performance: $250-500/month
pricing.notes: No free tier since 2022. Add-ons billed separately. Heroku Postgres from $5/month (hobby) to hundreds for production.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard HTTP error codes with JSON body; rate limit 429 with Retry-After header
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Some API endpoints support Idempotency-Key header for safe retries
known_agent_gotchas: Dyno formation scale-to-zero not native — dynos sleep on eco tier after 30min inactivity with cold start penalty
Release phase must complete successfully before app accepts traffic — track release status via releases endpoint
Config var changes trigger automatic restart — batch all env var changes in one PATCH request
Add-on attachment names differ from add-on plan names; use attachment name for DATABASE_URL env var lookup
Pipeline promotions via API don't wait for slug build — must poll pipeline-couplings for promotion completion

security_score_components.security_notes: Config vars encrypted at rest; Heroku Private Spaces for network isolation (additional cost). SOC2 Type II certified.
